title: althaea sylvestris
intro: Althaea sylvestris, also known as the althaea or marsh mallow, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and soothing effects due to its rich mucilage content, allowing it to form a protective barrier on the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of althaea sylvestris include its ability to treat digestive issues, soothe skin irritations, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as coughs and sore throats. Althaea sylvestris can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or syrup, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
